hero.title: Empowering Communities Through Education, Technology and Service
hero.tagline: Project Sankalp is a student-led social impact organization aligned with the National Service Scheme, dedicated to building leaders, bridging educational gaps, and serving communities with purpose.

about.title: Who We Are
about.subtitle: A student-led movement for lasting social change.
about.description:
Project Sankalp was born from a simple belief: that students are not just the leaders of tomorrow — they are the changemakers of today. Founded under the values of the National Service Scheme (NSS), we are a community of passionate young volunteers committed to education, social awareness, and community development.

From organizing awareness campaigns and skill-building workshops to conducting community drives and leadership programs, every initiative we undertake is rooted in empathy, service, and the collective will to create a better society. We don't just talk about change — we work for it, together.
about.mission: To empower students through service, cultivate leadership through action, and create lasting social impact through community-driven education and awareness programs.
about.vision: A society where every young person recognizes their responsibility to serve, lead, and uplift the communities around them.

values.title: Service Before Self
values.description: We believe in putting community needs above personal gain, embodying the spirit of the NSS motto: Not Me, But You.

values.title: Education as Empowerment
values.description: We treat education not as a privilege but as a right, working to make learning accessible to all sections of society.

values.title: Inclusive Leadership
values.description: We develop leaders who bring everyone along — especially the marginalized, the overlooked, and the unheard.

values.title: Technology for Good
values.description: We harness the power of technology as a tool for social good, bridging digital divides in underserved communities.

history.title: The Beginning
history.description: Project Sankalp starts as a small group of NSS volunteers committed to making a difference in local communities.

history.title: Community Outreach
history.description: Launched our first awareness campaigns, literacy drives, and community health initiatives reaching 500+ residents.

history.title: Institutional Growth
history.description: Expanded to multiple institutions, partnered with 10+ schools and NGOs, and launched the Tech for Good initiative.

history.title: Recognition & Scale
history.description: Recognized by the university NSS cell for outstanding social impact; crossed 300+ active volunteers.

history.title: The Mission Continues
history.description: Scaling our programs, reaching new communities, and building the next generation of compassionate leaders.

impact.title: Our Impact in Numbers
impact.subtitle: Every number represents a life touched, a community served, and a future changed.

caseStudies.title: Bridges to Byte: Digital Literacy in Nari Village
caseStudies.challenge: Lack of internet and digital equipment left over 300 youths in Nari village isolated from modern education systems during secondary schooling.
caseStudies.solution: Project Sankalp established a community digital lab equipped with 10 solar-powered computer terminals and trained 2 local graduates to run daily literacy classes.
caseStudies.result: Within 12 months, 180+ students graduated, 14 secured tech-based remote jobs, and the local secondary school integrated computer science into their core curriculum.

caseStudies.title: Krishi-Tech: Empowering Rural Farmers with IoT
caseStudies.challenge: Frequent crop failures due to unpredictable water table fluctuations and lack of affordable monitoring tools.
caseStudies.solution: Collaborated with tech university students to build low-cost IoT soil monitors and conducted hands-on workshops for 40 farming families.
caseStudies.result: Reduced water waste by 35%, increased overall crop yield by 20% in the first harvest, and trained 5 youth technicians for device maintenance.

initiatives.title: Sankalp Smart Labs
initiatives.description: We establish community computer labs in schools, community centers, and rural areas, providing students access to technology, the internet, and digital education that would otherwise be out of reach.
initiatives.impact: 35+ labs established across 12 cities, serving 8,000+ learners annually

initiatives.title: Tech Mentorship Program
initiatives.description: Connecting industry professionals with first-generation college students for structured mentorship, career guidance, portfolio development, and real-world skill building. One conversation can change a trajectory.
initiatives.impact: 500+ mentor-mentee pairs matched, 78% placed in internships or employment

initiatives.title: Community Awareness Drives
initiatives.description: Student volunteers lead campaigns on health, hygiene, environmental awareness, women's rights, and civic literacy. These drives bring knowledge directly to communities and mobilize grassroots action.
initiatives.impact: 150+ awareness campaigns conducted, 50,000+ community members engaged

events.title: Sankalp Leadership Summit 2025
events.description: A 2-day intensive leadership development program for student volunteers, featuring workshops on public speaking, project management, and social entrepreneurship.

events.title: Digital Safety Awareness Week
events.description: Week-long campaign educating school students on internet safety, cyberbullying prevention, and responsible social media use across 12 partner schools.

events.title: Hack for Good — Social Tech Hackathon
events.description: 48-hour hackathon where 120 students built technology solutions for real community problems — from crop monitoring apps to local health tracking systems.

events.title: Clean & Green Drive
events.description: 300+ volunteers participated in a community cleanup, tree-planting, and environmental awareness drive across 5 neighbourhoods.

events.title: Skill-Building Boot Camp
events.description: Intensive 3-day bootcamp teaching web development, graphic design, and content creation to first-generation college students with no prior exposure.

events.title: Sankalp Smart Lab #24 Inauguration
events.description: Launch of our 24th community digital lab in Nari Village, complete with solar-powered workstations and dedicated student coordinators.

programs.title: Ongoing Programs & Initiatives
programs.subtitle: Designed to empower, educate, and build long-term digital independence.

list.title: Sankalp Digital Literacy Academy
list.description: Bringing fundamental computer science skills, internet navigation, and safety training to school children and adults in remote regions.

list.title: Tech Mentorship & Career Accelerator
list.description: Connecting industry software engineers and designers with underprivileged college students for structured 1-on-1 mentorship, resume reviews, and portfolio development.

list.title: Sankalp Smart Lab Setup
list.description: Sourcing donated hardware, setting up physical computer labs in community centers, and supplying solar setups for continuous access.

list.title: Community Social Tech Fellows
list.description: A 6-month fellowship for young developers to build open-source local solutions, including health tracking and local business marketplace tools.

team.title: Meet the Team
team.subtitle: The passionate students and mentors behind Project Sankalp.

members.name: Dr. Ananya Iyer
members.bio: Social entrepreneur and educator with 15+ years of community development experience. Ananya leads with compassion and strategic clarity.

members.name: Vikram Rathore
members.bio: Operations leader who built Sankalp's volunteer network from the ground up. Vikram coordinates all institutional partnerships and field programs.

members.name: Sarah D'Souza
members.bio: Systems engineer specializing in off-grid technology. Sarah leads smart lab setups across rural areas with solar-powered local networks.

members.name: Dinesh Kumar
members.bio: Experienced educator and full-stack developer. Dinesh designs our learning programs and trains the next generation of student mentors.

partners.title: Our Partners & Supporters
partners.subtitle: Organizations that believe in our mission and amplify our impact.

list.name: EcoGrid Energy Systems
list.description: Provides solar panels and battery storage for our rural Smart Labs.

list.name: Apex University
list.description: Collaborates on student mentorship and volunteer recruitment.

list.name: Veloce Technologies
list.description: Donates laptops, monitors, and networking hardware for our labs.

list.name: Impact Foundation
list.description: Funder of our Community Social Tech Fellowship program.

list.name: National Service Scheme
list.description: Institutional alignment and recognition for our volunteer programs.

list.name: DigitalIndia Initiative
list.description: Policy support and infrastructure grants for digital inclusion.

stories.description: Members of Nari village and our volunteer team celebrate the launch of the new community hub.

stories.title: Mentorship Meetup 2025
stories.description: Connecting 50 industry mentors with college graduates for intensive review sessions.

stories.title: Sankalp Hack-For-Good

stories.title: First Digital Graduate
stories.description: Rohan proudly holds his course completion certificate in modern systems administration.

testimonials.quote: Project Sankalp changed how I see my role as a student. Volunteering here taught me that leadership is not about authority — it's about showing up for others.
testimonials.author: Priya Sharma

testimonials.quote: Running digital awareness campaigns with Sankalp gave me real-world skills that no classroom could. I learned to communicate, organize, and lead under pressure.
testimonials.author: Arjun Nair

testimonials.quote: Our school's partnership with Project Sankalp brought workshops that our students still talk about. The impact was immediate and genuine.
testimonials.author: Principal Jacob Thomas

testimonials.quote: I came to a Sankalp workshop expecting theory. What I got was a mentor who helped me land my first internship. This organization walks the talk.
testimonials.author: Meera Krishnan
